title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: Phrases like, "Can't you think about it one more time? I noticed some people are interested in me now that we've broken up," "I won't do this again, please give me one more chance," absolutely do not help in a successful reconciliation. In cases where reconciliation occurs through such words, the other person actually never intended to break up. They didn't say "let's break up" with the intention of breaking up; rather, they said it to "tame" you. Right now, the only thought in your head is likely that you must hold onto your partner somehow. That is why you will try

title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: d to break up. They didn't say "let's break up" with the intention of breaking up; rather, they said it to "tame" you. Right now, the only thought in your head is likely that you must hold onto your partner somehow. That is why you will try to talk about the future—how much I loved you and how much I will love you in the future, what I will do for you, and how I will change my behavior. However, such future-oriented talk does not move emotions. These kinds of conversations are closer to persuasion. Saying, "I will give you these benefits in the future," might evoke anticipation for the future, but it is not the first thing you should say in a breakup situation where emotions need to be moved. Ultimately, don't you have to persuade the other person? That is correct. However, there are two types of persuasion: persuasion in non-emotional relationships and persuasion in emotional relationships. Examples of persuasion in non-emotional relationships include business negotiations, haggling over prices, and lawsuits. In situations like this, we can succeed in persuasion as long as our logic is solid. However, persuasion in emotional relationships is different. Rather than logic, rational persuasion is needed only after the listener's heart has been moved. Reconciliation is no different. That is why the future-oriented talk you are about to share is not something you need to discuss right now. So, how exactly do you get the other person to come back? First, you have to stir their emotions. So, how exactly do you do that? I am going to tell you right now. If you use the methods I am sharing today, the other person might not react the moment they hear it, but they will feel uncomfortable constantly—while sleeping at night, walking down the street, or looking at their phone. Eventually, they will come back to you. No, wait. I will make you regret saying that five minutes later. The first method is reminding them of the meaning. Think about moments when we feel heartbroken. When a dog we raised for a long time dies, or when someone close to us passes away. The reason we feel pain in these moments is that we look back on the past and realize the meaning of that person's existence. " My Coco always looked only at me and loved me, but I could n't give back that much." At times like this, we become incredibly sad, and we think that if only we could turn back time, we would do it even if it meant shortening our own lifespan. The other person feels the

title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: me and loved me, but I could n't give back that much." At times like this, we become incredibly sad, and we think that if only we could turn back time, we would do it even if it meant shortening our own lifespan. The other person feels the same way. When you realize the true meaning of your relationship, your heart melts, and you begin to rethink your current relationship. For couples considering divorce, hearing words like, "Let's compromise and make things work," or " I will put in this much effort for you," does not mean the divorce is finalized. It is when you hear a sad yet poignant meaning of the relationship—such as, "I am still grateful to you," " Besides my parents, no one in my life has been as precious as you," or " Even if we break up, you will still be precious to me"—that your heart begins to soften. However, there is one mistake many of you make at this point. You might think, "In a way, what I just said is no different from saying, 'I love you, so please reconsider.'" There are two problems with this approach. First, it is difficult for the other person to grasp the significance of your relationship solely through the words "I love you." In fact, "I love you" is such a common phrase that it does n't really resonate when heard in the middle of a breakup. Therefore, when saying this,

title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: person to grasp the significance of your relationship solely through the words "I love you." In fact, "I love you" is such a common phrase that it does n't really resonate when heard in the middle of a breakup. Therefore, when saying this, you must use expressions that make the other person think, "This person loved me this much—I was so oblivious to it"—something they have never heard before. Second, you should not use language in the present tense. Rather than present tense sentences like, "You are so precious to me"... Past tense sentences like "You were so precious to me" are expressions that pierce the other person's heart much more painfully. It may be a difference of just one word—or rather, one letter— but it adds the meaning that "I am very sad, but because I respect your position, I will accept this situation." You might say that if I say this now, you think I have let go of the relationship and you are afraid the other person will completely move on, but the chances of reconciliation increase significantly. The second point is proving your worth. I won't make cliché suggestions like telling you to upload a pretty profile picture because they are so common. Honestly speaking, no matter how pretty I become, it is difficult to be more attractive than the new people of the opposite sex around that person. Especially from the perspective of someone who says they are "completely fed up with me," no matter how pretty I become now, it is hard to resonate unless I suddenly become celebrity-level beautiful. Of course, it is obviously better to do it than not, but making this our main weapon is like playing soccer 22 against 11 on a tilted playing field. So what exactly should I show? It is not about showing, but making them realize. Because we were lovers, there are advantages that other people of the opposite sex all have. For instance, understanding the other person's personal problems more, being able to care for them like family, or the other person's

title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: them realize. Because we were lovers, there are advantages that other people of the opposite sex all have. For instance, understanding the other person's personal problems more, being able to care for them like family, or the other person's You emphasize the advantages of being a long-term partner, such as being able to accept their selfish side or catering to their trivial tastes that are hard to know unless you've been together for a long time. There is no need to overthink it; simply emphasize the point mentioned in point 1 above, and then speak as if you are talking about the good things about your relationship. For example, you could say, "What I liked about you compared to other men was that, at least with you, I did n't let my pride get in the way and could show my weakest sides. Now that I'm older, I've found someone like family with whom I can tell things I can't even tell my parents." You could say, "There was someone who understood what I like and when I struggle—things you would n't know unless you spent a long time together." While it may appear as if you are expressing your gratitude, it is actually a way to make the other person realize why this relationship is more precious to you than a new romance or a new person. The third point is to stay quiet. You might naturally think, "Just sit there sucking your thumb and fail." However, it does n't literally mean staying quiet. Even after breaking up, the other person will likely be curious about your feelings. Unless you had a massive fight, blocked each other, and broke up in a chaotic mess, people who initiated the breakup often stalk your Instagram or KakaoTalk. In those moments, [you might see] my current struggles and depression on Instagram or KakaoTalk Anyone who has watched at least three reunion videos is likely already aware that you shouldn't reveal your emotions exactly as they are. That is why you will likely try

title: 헤어지고 이 3가지를 하면 돌아올 사람은 알아서 돌아옵니다.
text: ght see] my current struggles and depression on Instagram or KakaoTalk Anyone who has watched at least three reunion videos is likely already aware that you shouldn't reveal your emotions exactly as they are. That is why you will likely try to show your partner photos on social media of you doing well, having fun with friends, and smiling broadly. However, honestly speaking, it is better not to make any changes to your KakaoTalk profile or social media for the first three weeks or so. If you broke up yesterday and suddenly show yourself having a blast with friends or at a drinking party today, people who still have feelings might feel a sense of inexplicable jealousy. However, those who are still seriously contemplating the breakup might lose all affection at such a sight and completely cut off any remaining feelings. Up to about three weeks, showing these sides of yourself wo n't be a problem from your partner's perspective. To be frank, we can't just stay cooped up at home crying and hugging our stomachs just because we broke up, can we? So, from this point on, it is fine to upload content focusing on you doing well, just as you have in mind. However, there is one important concept here: the concept of "double clues." You should mix in a few clues showing your emotional ups and downs in between showing yourself doing well after the breakup. The human brain [is uncertain] When they see you, it sparks curiosity. In other words, regardless of whether the other person is thinking about reconciliation or not, you must confuse their feelings for them to become curious. This leads to them contacting you one more time, checking your Instagram one more time, or spending more time thinking about you. If you only show that you are doing well or that you are constantly struggling, curiosity won't arise because both are clearly defined. However, even if there seem to be no issues in your daily life, if they see you appearing to be struggling every once in a while, they will pay attention to you the next day and the day after that, if only out of curiosity. As that time increases, the likelihood of a backlash or feelings of longing for you naturally rises. Do you understand? You should consider that you must do these three things immediately after breaking up, without overthinking. You never needed to think things like, "Should I go see them?" "Is the other person waiting?" "Will things change if I write a sincere letter?" in the first place. If you are watching this video shortly after a breakup, make sure to do these three things first. Then, visit the Love Language Research Institute website linked in this video and read the "Reconciliation Algorithm" PDF. It shows how to rule out situations that don't fall under today's methods. I have prepared all the available methods. Since I am giving them away for free anyway, anyone who doesn't check them out would be missing out. Go check it out and take a look.
